title: NASA Demonstrates New Prescribed Burn Capability for Spaceport

title: CEQ Permitting Innovators Program Call for Solutions
summary: NASA’s Center of Excellence for Collaborative Innovation (CoECI) assists in the use of crowdsourcing across the federal government. CoECI’s NASA Tournament Lab offers the contract capability to run external crowdsourced challenges on behalf of NASA and other agencies. title: House Appropriators Poised to Reject Proposed NASA Budget Cuts
summary: The House Appropriations committee is poised once again to reject the deep cuts to NASA proposed by the Trump Administration for FY2027. The Commerce-Justice-Science subcommittee is recommending $24.4 billion, the […]

title: Plasma-hot Space Rider tests for belly and flaps
Stunning images from Biomass mark its one year in orbit
summary: To mark the first anniversary of the European Space Agency’s Biomass satellite, we present a selection of striking images captured over the past 12 months, revealing Earth’s forests, and much more, in new detail. In just one year, this pioneering mission has begun transforming our understanding of forest dynamics and advancing how scientists monitor the critical role forests play in regulating the global carbon cycle.

title: A Falcon 9 upper stage will strike the Moon in August
summary: The object will be traveling at 2.43 km a second, or 5,400 mph, upon impact.

title: Fires Rage in Georgia
summary: Firefighters are battling two destructive blazes in the southern part of the state as drought grips the U.S. Southeast.

title: Live coverage: SpaceX seeks second attempt at Falcon Heavy launch following weather scrub on Monday
summary: The mission will send the ViaSat-3 F3 satellite to a geosynchronous transfer orbit. Liftoff from Launch Complex 39A at NASA’s Kennedy Space Center is scheduled for 10:13 a.m. EDT (1413 UTC), at the opening of an 85-minute window.

title: NASA Connects Little Red Dots with Chandra, Webb
summary: A newly discovered object may be a key to unlocking the true nature of a mysterious class of sources that astronomers have found in the early universe in recent years. A “X-ray dot” found by NASA’s Chandra X-ray Observatory could explain what the hundreds or potentially thousands of these objects are. A paper describing the […]
